seed_problem: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost
mutation: input: relax the covering domain from a tree to a general grid graph with impassable obstacles (cycles + distorted, non-tree coverage balls) and require covering EVERY habitable cell, making the weighted min-cost cover NP-hard
title: Polar Research Base: Thermal Relay Placement
strategies: trivial baseline: install a relay on every habitable sector (cost = baseline B)
max-coverage greedy: repeatedly install the relay warming the most still-cold sectors (ignoring cost), ties to lower id
cost-effectiveness greedy: repeatedly install the relay maximizing newly-warmed sectors per unit cost, then prune relays made redundant
multi-start local search: seeded randomized cost-effectiveness greedy over several restarts, redundancy pruning, keep the cheapest feasible cover
